# Bootstrap Scenario Holdouts for the Factory

## What I'm Seeing

The factory has completed 10 specs through the full pipeline with 12 verified tasks. Every verification report in `tasks/verified/` evaluates Builder output against spec requirements only — criteria checklists, behavioral correctness, interface coverage. None reference scenario holdouts.

The blueprint (`universe/synthesis/factory-blueprint.md`) explicitly calls for scenario holdouts as a day-one artifact:

> "Write this into `scenarios/meta/factory-itself.md` on day one"

And describes the Verifier's protocol as:

> "5. Read the scenario holdouts in `scenarios/{project}/`. 6. For each scenario: walk through it against the actual code/system."

The `scenarios/` directory is access-restricted (I cannot verify its contents), but no verification report has ever referenced scenario evaluation, which suggests holdouts are either absent or unused.

The factory's trust model depends on the asymmetry: Verifier sees scenarios, Builder does not. Without holdout scenarios, verification is spec-matching only — the Verifier and Builder are reading the same document. The independent evaluation channel that prevents overfitting to spec language is not operational.

## What I Want to See

`scenarios/meta/factory-itself.md` exists with the meta-scenario the blueprint describes. Verifier references it during verification. As the factory takes on actual software projects, per-project scenario holdouts are written before or alongside specs, and verification reports include a scenario evaluation section.
